title: Chettinad Karaikudi Egg Roast

subs.name: 4 pieces of firm paneer (50 g each), pan-fried until golden
reduce to 0.5 tsp for medium heat

steps.text: Score each peeled egg with 4 shallow cuts running from end to end — this lets the oil and spice penetrate the surface. Pat the eggs dry with kitchen paper.

Heat 2 tablespoons of gingelly oil in a kadai over medium-high heat. Add the eggs and fry, rolling them constantly, until the outside is golden and blistered all over, about 4 minutes. Remove the eggs and set aside.
In the same kadai, add the remaining tablespoon of oil. Add fennel seeds and curry leaves. Add shallots and fry on medium heat until deeply golden and fragrant, about 5 minutes.

Add ginger-garlic paste and fry 1 minute. Add the tomato and cook for 3 minutes until it breaks down. Lower the flame and add red chilli powder, coriander powder, turmeric, black pepper and salt. Stir for 30 seconds.

Return the fried eggs to the kadai. Gently fold them into the masala, coating every surface. Add 2 tablespoons of water if the masala is sticking. Cook on medium for 3 minutes until the masala clings tightly to the eggs.
Scatter coriander leaves and serve immediately with idli, dosa, parotta or rice. The fried-egg surface hardens quickly, so do not leave this dish standing.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
critical_checks: Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: egg → 4 pieces of firm paneer (50 g each), pan-fried until golden; gingelly-oil → coconut oil or neutral oil; shallot → 1 large onion, finely chopped; black-pepper → reduce to 0.5 tsp for medium heat
